extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSON Mads won bunch sprint after break caught inside 200m; CLARK and DEMARCHI caught after waiting too long in final 800 meters.
tactical_patterns: Clark-Demarchi duo hesitated repeatedly inside 800m despite 33-second gap at 3km, allowing bunch sprint catch.
Thomas mechanical inside 15km required team time trial through cars, exposing GC vulnerability on disorganized sprint stages.
Milan prioritized sprint points over stage win, finishing second to consolidate ciclamino rather than launching earlier.

rider_signals.CLARK Simon: negative: 36-year-old had winning legs but lost tactical nerve in final 800m by waiting too long
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped on first categorized climb due to knee injury from stage 5 crash, unable to contest sprint
rider_signals.SVENDGAARD Mads Pedersen: negative: missing from commentary despite sprint finish, suggesting poor form or positioning
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: losing time on easier stages suggests fragility beyond stage 5 incident
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: mechanical inside 15km exposed GC risk, needed teammates to pace back through convoy
